text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
A winning concert is a stage of<n>dreams. Every
runner longs to stand<n>in the spotlight and sing
"thank you"<n>to the fans who cheered for them.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
But that dream only comes true for a<n>select few.
The harsh reality is that<n>many will only ever stand
on stage as<n>backup dancers.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
One day during concert practice, Smart<n>Falcon's
classmate expresses<n>disdain for learning backup
dancer<n>choreography.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Falcon makes the case for giving<n>100% no matter
who's in the spotlight<n>as a way of showing
appreciation to<n>the fans, but her plea falls on deaf
ears.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
They all dream of standing center<n>stage. The
glamor of the spotlight<n>wouldn't be the same if
they didn't<n>have to earn it, but fierce competition
also breeds tension.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Falcon begins to imagine a world<n>without centers
or backup dancers,<n>one in which they could all
stand on<n>stage as equals.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
That's when Light Hello shows up.<n>Calling herself
an event producer, she<n>pitches a plan to bring
back a<n>different type of concert—the Grand
Concert!
